explanations.2: The 'source' command loads another shell script file so we can use the functions defined in it, similar to an import statement.
explanations.4: This creates an array (a list) of command-line arguments that we'll pass to the function we're testing.
explanations.6: The 'run' keyword executes the function and captures its output so we can check what it printed; ${arrArguments[@]} means 'all elements of the array'.
explanations.8: This checks that the function's output contains the specified text somewhere in it, rather than requiring an exact match.
